title: Do You Need an Architect for a Scarsdale Bathroom Renovation?
excerpt: A lot of Scarsdale homeowners wonder if hiring an architect is worth it for a bathroom remodel. The answer depends on what you're trying to do—and who's running the project.
content:
When a Bathroom Project Gets Complicated

Not every bathroom renovation needs an architect. If you're replacing fixtures, updating tile, and refreshing the vanity within the existing footprint, a general contractor alone can handle it just fine.

But if you're moving walls, relocating plumbing or electrical lines, changing the ceiling height, or adding a second sink and separate shower where there wasn't room before, you've crossed into territory where design matters. That's where an architect becomes useful—or even necessary.

Scarsdale Has Real Building Requirements

Like every municipality in Westchester, Scarsdale has its own building department and permit process. Before you touch anything structural, you need to file plans and get approval. The building inspector wants to see that the work meets code and makes sense from a safety and structural standpoint.

If you're only refreshing surfaces, permits might be straightforward. But the moment you're altering the layout or structure of the bathroom, you need drawings. Those drawings need to come from someone who knows how to make them—and someone who understands how the bathroom fits into the rest of your home's mechanical systems.

The Advantage of Having Both Under One Roof

Here's what most homeowners in Scarsdale don't realize: you can hire a single professional who is both a licensed architect in New York and a licensed general contractor in Westchester. That person can design the bathroom, pull the permits, and build it themselves.

That matters because there's no handoff between different companies. No miscommunication. No finger-pointing if something doesn't fit right. One person sees the job from start to finish and takes responsibility for the whole thing.

Silvio Luca runs his projects this way. He's a licensed architect and general contractor based in Eastchester, working throughout Westchester County including Scarsdale. When he designs your bathroom, he's thinking about it the way a builder thinks—not just as a pretty rendering, but as something that has to actually get built, fit together, and work.

Why This Matters for Your Scarsdale Home

A Scarsdale home—especially an older one—has quirks. Maybe the walls aren't perfectly square. Maybe the floor slopes slightly. Maybe the plumbing is in an odd location. A residential architect who also builds knows how to work around these real-world issues instead of creating designs that look good on paper but create headaches on site.

When you combine design expertise with construction experience, you get a bathroom that's beautiful, functional, and actually buildable in your specific house.

The Real Question

Ask yourself: am I changing the layout or structure? If yes, you need an architect's drawings to pull permits. If you're working with someone who is both a licensed architect in Westchester and a general contractor, you're getting the best of both worlds.

If you're unsure whether your Scarsdale bathroom project needs architectural design, call (914) 224-7383 for a free consultation. Silvio can walk you through what you're planning and tell you exactly what it will take.
